what_it_does: This file is a research and build-plan document that maps how to unify the project's stream/guided-wizard mechanism with the existing intake and setup engine, listing what already exists, what to reuse, and a sequence of incremental build steps.
observations: The author identifies MS_INTAKE as the adaptive engine pattern that streams should generalize rather than replace.
It lists already-built pieces such as MS_LAUNCH, MS_RESOLVE, MS_STREAMS, KindSpecimen, StreamHost, and StreamRunner.
It proposes reusing the intake.jsx QuestionBody renderers for stream 'ask' steps to avoid duplicating UI.
It argues streams should be embedded inline inside real workspace pages (Generation, Review, Build, Operations, Sequence) instead of living on a separate Streams page.
It documents the generation API surface from MS_SYNTH_AI for real 'produce' steps.
It includes a risk-managed unification decision to keep MS_INTAKE intact and generalize MS_STREAMS incrementally.
It ends with concrete build increments I1–I4 and mandates TDD, token-driven development, loud failure, schema mirroring, and CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md updates.
standouts: The central pivot away from a standalone Streams page toward inline, context-aware guided wizards embedded where work happens.
The decision to reuse MS_INTAKE's adaptive move/area model and QuestionBody renderers as the common stream step vocabulary.
The explicit risk-managed stance of incrementally converging WizardScreen with the stream runner rather than big-bang refactoring.
conventions: Follows incremental planning with numbered build increments and explicit research-before-build checkpoints.
Follows project conventions around TDD, token-driven development, loud failure, schema mirroring, and documentation in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
Breaks the earlier assumption that MS_STREAMS should be a fixed linear list of text steps.
concerns: The author self-flags scope/risk as large and warns against a big-bang refactor.
Exporting module-scope renderers from intake.jsx may carry closure dependencies that are not yet analyzed.
The plan depends on several cross-module APIs (MS_SYNTH_AI, MS_LAUNCH, MS_RESOLVE, MS_MEMORY) staying stable during the increments.
notes: A research plan that reframes streams as adaptive, embedded wizards built by reusing the existing intake engine and visual generation pipeline.
questions: What exact shape will the generalized MS_STREAMS step/move object take?
How will the understanding/gate scoring be defined for non-project-init streams?
How will the embedded stream runner behave when Claude is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to plan the unification of streams and setup; the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Visual generation as a first-class step output
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to evolve MS_STREAMS from a naive linear step list into an adaptive, visually generating, context-aware guidance engine that can live inside any project page, while reusing the proven intake patterns and renderers.
novelty: It treats the existing project-setup engine (MS_INTAKE) as the canonical adaptive-interaction pattern and proposes embedding stream guidance directly into real workspace pages via MS_LAUNCH context rather than keeping streams as a separate page.
contribution: It contributes a unification strategy and concrete build increments that connect the existing intake renderers, stream runner, launch context, and synthetic generation pipeline into a single adaptive guidance system.
summary_for_embedding: A research and build plan for unifying the project's guided streams with the existing adaptive intake engine, reusing intake renderers, embedding streams inline in real workspace pages, and incrementally converging with project init.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to plan the next build cycle for making streams adaptive, visual, and embedded across the app while reusing existing engine components.
